---
title: "Self-Study — Overview"
---

**What the self-study period is for:**

- Practice AI-assisted coding on your own, at your own pace
- Get comfortable with the Positron + Copilot workflow before Day 2
- Accumulate specific questions and observations to bring back to the group

---

## How It Works

The self-study period runs for **2–3 days** between the two sessions.
You work independently through the structured case studies below and document your experience.

### Teams channel

A live Teams channel is open throughout the self-study period.
Use it to ask questions, share screenshots of what worked or didn't, and pick up tips from colleagues.

*Placeholder — add Teams channel link here.*

### Submission

At the end of the self-study period you are expected to **submit a completed case study**
showing how you used AI assistance. This is one of the two course deliverables.

- Submit via *placeholder — add submission link/instructions*
- Deadline: *placeholder — before Session 2 start*
- Format: a brief write-up (half a page) + the code you produced

::: callout-note
You do not need to submit a perfect result. Honest documentation of what worked, what failed, and what questions you have is more valuable than a polished but uncritical submission.
:::

---

## Suggested Approach

1. Pick **one case study** from the list below that is closest to your current work
2. Set up the exercise files in a local folder (no OneDrive paths with spaces — see [Common Problems](../prerequisites/common-problems.qmd))
3. Attempt the task first without AI to have a baseline
4. Repeat with AI assistance and note the differences
5. Write up your observations and open questions

---

## Case Studies

| Case study | Best for | Link |
|------------|---------|------|
| Reproducing old code with new data | Anyone with legacy scripts | [→](case-studies.qmd#reproducing-old-code) |
| Reproducing an analysis from an example | New to a topic or language | [→](case-studies.qmd#reproducing-from-example) |
| Trying a different language (R or Python instead of Stata) | Stata users wanting to expand | [→](case-studies.qmd#trying-a-different-language) |
